verses.text: The LORD: come up, thou and Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, and seventy of the elders; worship afar. Moses alone shall come near; the people shall not come up. Guidance: Even on a holy mountain there are circles of nearness. Most worship from a distance; some elders come closer; one mediator approaches alone. God's nearness is graded — but it has always required a mediator.

The LORD: come up, thou and Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, and seventy of the elders; worship afar. Moses alone shall come near; the people shall not come up. Guidance: Even on a holy mountain there are circles of nearness. Most worship from a distance; some elders come closer; one mediator approaches alone. God's nearness is graded — but it has always required a mediator.

Moses told the people all the words of the LORD; the people answered with one voice: all the LORD has said, we will do. Moses wrote the words; rose early, built an altar under the hill, and twelve pillars for the twelve tribes. Guidance: The people say yes a second time, with one voice. Moses writes it down so the vow is no longer just remembered, it is recorded. Faithfulness needs ink.

Moses told the people all the words of the LORD; the people answered with one voice: all the LORD has said, we will do. Moses wrote the words; rose early, built an altar under the hill, and twelve pillars for the twelve tribes. Guidance: The people say yes a second time, with one voice. Moses writes it down so the vow is no longer just remembered, it is recorded. Faithfulness needs ink.

He sent young men of the children of Israel, who offered burnt offerings and sacrificed peace offerings of oxen unto the LORD. Guidance: Burnt offerings give the whole animal; peace offerings end in a shared meal. The covenant begins with surrender and ends at a table — a pattern that holds throughout Scripture.

Moses took half the blood for basins; half he sprinkled on the altar. He read the book of the covenant; the people said: all the LORD has said, we will do and obey. Moses sprinkled the blood on the people: behold the blood of the covenant. Guidance: Half the blood on the altar — for God. Half on the people — for them. Jesus uses these exact words at the Last Supper to seal a new covenant.

Moses, Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, and seventy elders went up; they saw the God of Israel — under His feet, sapphire pavement, like the body of heaven in clearness. He laid not His hand on the nobles; they saw God, and did eat and drink. Guidance: They saw God and lived. They ate and drank in His presence. Heaven's floor is sapphire — a glimpse Ezekiel and John will later see again.

The LORD: come up to Me; I will give thee tables of stone, the law I have written. Moses rose with Joshua his minister and went up. To the elders: tarry till we return; Aaron and Hur are with you — any matter, come to them. Guidance: Moses delegates wisely. Aaron and Hur stand as visible authorities; Joshua is being trained as the next leader. Healthy leaders plan for their absence.

Moses went up; a cloud covered the mount. The glory of the LORD abode on Sinai six days. The seventh day He called Moses from the cloud. To Israel, His glory looked like devouring fire on the top. Moses entered the cloud and was on the mount forty days and nights. Guidance: Six days of silence in the cloud before God speaks. Even Moses waited. Don't read divine silence as divine absence.
